reviews.review_msg: Honestly, If you want to actually learning Marketing Research method, I would like recommend you take David's course. He is a nice person and his lecture is well organize. You will learn how to use SPSS and basic marketing research methods. Be ready to prepare the lecture notes before you come, because you have to take pop quiz. Exams are easy.

The class is not necessarily difficult, but it requires A LOT of work. For the 2nd half of the semester, be prepared to learn class material/SPSS by yourself and spend hours on assignments that are literally worth 5%.  Also be prepared for life stories and overall weird/uncomfortable vibes.

The first half was alright, but the second half was absolutely brutal. Weekly assignments required more effort than they are worth. Prof focused too much on basic topics and not enough on complicated topics.... Unless you want to reteach yourself EVERYTHING from BUEC 232 and BUS 336 without any help at all, DON'T DO THIS CLASS WITH DAVID!

DON'T DO IT. Unless you understand how to use SPSS or are already practiced in Marketing Research, this professor sucks. He goes on and on about irrelevant stories, and doesn't teach ANYTHING, especially when it comes to the more difficult statistical topics. FIND. SOMEONE. ELSE.

Save yourself the time and take it with another prof. David's assignments are worth a minimal amount but are a) marked hard b) no criteria is given c) take way too much time for what they're worth of your grade. He also does not teach course material in class but rather talks about irrelevant life stories. For the sake of your sanity, don't do it

dobson is OKAY. when you email him i guess depending on his mood he will either send you a full letter or one word response. I'm not sure about 336, but he's EZ for 345 but a lot of work etc. he is very enthused about stats to say the least but i haven't seen him yelling at anyone thus far.

Worst prof I've ever had. I am willing to overlook the accent, but regardless of the accent he is a terrible teacher.  Fortunately the course is easy, did not attend a whole class after the first midterm and still got an A-.  He did make the final ridiculously tricky.  Super rigid about marks,way too worried about cheating. Truly terrible professor

Despite his problems in English, if you ask him for help he will take the time to reinterpret the material! He's so enthusiastic which will honestly make you love  the class! His tests are easy -no surprises, just do the homework and read his notes. Awesome teacher!
HONESTLY, GET ANOTHER PROF..

Unclear, he sounds like Kermit the frog with an Indian accent, couldn't understand even when sitting at the front. Also rambles way too much about cheating, used half a lecture and got pissed off when students asked questions about it. His notes were based on an older version of the text so you'd have to find out on your on when to stop reading...

midterm had one queston on beta value, 3-5 questions on the probability. study that  probability!!i studied 2 weeks ahead for finals...dont have to read the ENTIRE book but just make sure u go thru the notes and do one or two practice examples. the models are really important make sure u know how to write those equations and nodes!

Very easy material. The depth of the material is very surface (compared to STAT 285). Lack of mathmatical proofs = No way for students to truly understand the material (intead of simply memorizing when to use which formula) and apply them later for serious quantitative research. Lectures are long and boring. Labs are too crowded.
Keep avoid him by all cost. the material should be easy but he just makes it hard~! his teaching style is terrible and the lecture is useless and boring.
Dobson is a very good teacher if you want to clown around dont take his class but if you want to learn something he will help you learn very useful material.
Dobson is the worst prof, bad teaching style, really rude when you ask him question. Not recommand!!! I Take Bus 336 with Michael Johnson!!!Awesome Prof!!! I regret take Bus232 with Dobson

I can't believe the faculty hires such a bad prof. His lectures are completely useless, you will do much better if you just study by yourself. Take 336 with someone else. I regret wasting time and money on him.

One of the worst profs at SFU. It's not to say he isn't knowledgable, but he has NO IDEA how to teach. He rambles on about useless stuff the whole time, and can't really speak English. Get another prof!!!

He knows his stuff, but he sucks at teaching it. Assignments rely heavily on Excel, which are useless because they can't be asked in a written exam. Basically you need to guess how he will test you in the exam, and then study by teaching yourself based on his slides which don't give enough detail on what the hell is going on.

Agree with previous comment. This prof don't like to help students with questions. I saw him insult student in the lecture.Assignments are much harder compare to the other 336 class. Teaching is too emotional and speaking is not clear. Not a good professor.

Really terrible prof, Worst Prof I've ever seen. Heavy Indian accent with fast and unclear speaking. what a nightmare. He loves to pick on students and insult students with his bad tempter. You better not ask him question. This guy is not encourage for question.(Specailly he thinks you should know it)Avoid him if possible. Terrible crazy pro.
Terrible prof. He takes forever to start talking about the material and wastes half the class blabbing about how great it's going to be. Avoid him if possible.
This is to correct the rating from the previous comment - I mistakenly posted it while having a wrong rating. Avoid this guy.

This guy is terrible.He's rude,and really takes pleasure in picking on students.I tried asking him for help once,and he was extremely dismissive started making gratuitous comments about how I didn't know my stuff.Although the course material is fairly easy,you'll have to put in extra effort just to put up with his ****.
useless professor,unclear and have indian accent, very unhelpful, do not go to his class because it is just waste of time. Never take his class
he is one of the best profs I have ever had; tough but fair
In one word - atrocious. Does SFU Business even care?

not a bad prof,material are easy,avg is about 75%, so it's too easy to be curved down,have to study the notes very hard memorize every words in his notes and fully understand the sample questions.make sure you have found the trap in every question before writing down your answer.

Dobson is one of the best profs at SFU.  Yeah, he can be repetitive but that is because he wants you to understand the material. He makes boring stuff intersting. He is always available to help and answer questions.  Funny, too!

In Buec232, Dobson was a clear and entertaining teacher. Yet, in Bus336, I found him to be over repetitive and boring enough to put me to a nap sometimes. Each class should take roughly around 30min to teach at the most, but he spreads it out to a painful 2.5-3 hrs span.  Assignments take around 2 hrs to complete. MT and Final was challenging.

i don't get the previous poster's deal with dobson's accent.90% percent of the students in the class have accents.i think you're just pissed 'cuz you didn't do well.don't blame the prof 'cuz you don't know your stuff.Dobson is clear, focused, structured, entertaining, but does not care for slackers. AWESOME PROF!!!

I thought my final could get 50/50, but i did not. I know his exams are always really tricky and you may never know which Qs you got wrong. Anyway, I got an A again (buec232 also). Easy course, boring class!

Questions on exams were not as easy as they seemed bcuz I got lower than expected.Light workload though.In BUEC 232, I thought he was just over-enthusiastic, but in 336 I found his teaching style even more annoying.By shouting, running around and repeating useless stuff, he makes it complicated.He should have changed his accent instead of his name.

Do not let his Anglo-Saxon name deceive you! He has a very thick accent; I can barely understand him. Lectures are way too long (3hrs). Tutorials useless too. The textbook is amazing though, you can skip 3hr class and learn just as much by reading for 30min. It teaches Excel by steps too (instead of memorizing what TA's do in tutorial).

Listen to me: if you have to take 336, take it with someone else. Every Wednesday when I had to sit 3 hours in his class, I learnt NOTHING. By the end of the semester I was so annoyed of his awful jokes and his useless class. The fact that he makes all his exams MC is also ridiculous. Save yourself some frustration and take it with someone else.

This guy would be a good prof if he improved his clarity. The way he presents the information could be a lot more straight forward. Not only that, but he has an extremely thick accent that can be quite difficult to understand.

to the comment below me: HAHA! wow, you seem pretty upset about the whole scantron thing.. i bet that person who got yelled at by dobson was YOU   ... anyways, in my opinion, dobson is an OKAY professor.. he seems pretty lazy when it comes to answering student questions.. but he goes over the material pretty well.. the exams are pretty fair too

i don't care how enthusiastic he is or how many times he says "i'm not kidding", this guy is a fricken lunatic and loves to yell at people for just about anything. i think he actually takes pleasure in making an ass of himself. screamed at a guy for filling in a scantron before exam started. actually ranted for about a minute

One of the best and funniest prof ever. However the clarity can be a problem. He has an accent and talks fast. Must attend lectures because its very useful when you do your homework and as study materials for exams. Exams are average difficulty.
Know your theory for MT and final.  I cannot stress enough.   Everything else is easy.

Lectures aren't useful, he doesn't explain clearly. Assignment questions kinda similar to exam but too time-consuming considering how much they're worth. Midterm & final were multiple-choice and ~40% based on theory rather than calculations. Textbook is useful if u wanna clearly understand concepts, but exams are based solely on online ppt slides.

I am just surprised by all the comments here. Like OMG, i bet all these people are pissed because they never bothered to study and screwed up on the midterm. David is an awesome prof. If you show interest towards what he is teaching, then we will help you to the best of his ability. I agree lectures were BORING but hey, thats stats for you!

Makes easy concepts hard to understand.  Imagine having to turn to an old textbook in order to get clarification on what a PROF is saying, shouldn't it be the other way around?  He tries, I'll give him that- he's just not cut out for teaching.

Oh David. Seems like a nice guy as a quirky uncle or something, but can be a big **** in class, making fun of or getting pissed at students for trivial things. Not a great lecturer, a bit hard to understand, lecturing style is annoying (says the same stuff over and over again). TAs were USELESS and textbook was not needed READ LECTURE NOTES!!!

Absolute psycho in the first half of the course. He literally went from happy one second to yelling at students for minutes because of the dumbest and smallest reasons. He got nicer by the end of the semester after people reported him to the Dean. Sit in the back for a quick exit. You probably won't pay attention for a good chunk.

You'll get tired of his "GOOD MORNING CLASS. TODAY IS A GOOD DAY" after the first week. High energy guy, but it gets repetitive and annoying. He takes absolutely forever to go over simple material. Stay home and read the book. Sit in the nose-bleeds unless you want to get picked on.

He was probably one of the worst profs I have encountered in SFU. He goes over his lectures too quickly and he often skipped a bunch! Bus 336 was based on learning excel but he did all the excel stuff too quickly during class. He was horribleeee!

Material is easy enough when he goes over it in class but that's because he teaches in a very glossy sort of way. Do the homework and readings and you should be fine.  Midterm was expected to be easy but was very challenging.

good evening~ say to ur neighbor "it is soooooooo  nice to meet u tonight"~ u guys are amazing and i am sure u will have a great time tonight~~2 reasons~u guys choose to be here,instead,u could be at home,and watch American idol,because u guys choose to come,so u can say to urself after tonight's lecture, I LEARN SOMETHING NEW! i am not kidding~~

Picks on students EVERY class, always stopping lecture mid session and start screaming at random students for the littlest things.. but hasn't been a real issue for me until he started screaming at me and my friend out of the blue for not having my notebook out and for being "ridiculous, sitting there doing nothing", we weren't even talking!!
He would try his best to get to know his students, he would try to remember active students' names. you could see his passion being a teacher and passing his knowledge to his generation.
takes lollipops as class examples...so funny....and each of us get a free lolliopop at the end.....!!!!!!!!!verry great prof~~~ "im not kidding"

First 30 minutes of lecture was boring. Exams were easy. Didn't do any of the questions in the book for exams. Know his notes inside and out. Text was good reference when notes didn't make sense.Final started 35 mins late. 95%+ for A+. If highest is 80, that one person gets A-. Overall, a pretty easy class. Goodluck! =]

MCs exam, each question worth 1% towards the grade. Though it's easy, but you know, the class is curved. He gave lollipops to the evening class, is that bribery? And yeah "TODAY IS A GOOD DAY,IM NOT KIDDING!"

"TODAY IS A GOOD DAY,IM NOT KIDDING"..he says this at the start of every class!! he's extremely hilarious! he knows what he's teaching and he loves it!! his midterm and final are both MCs!! and its very easy!! just study his lecture notes and u will definitely do good in this class!!!

Great prof. Very friendly and very funny. Makes the most unenjoyable class interesting for you. He's an energetic guy that's passionate about what he teaches. Attend class, read the notes, and do the practices in the book for sure. Definitely recommended! "I'm not kidding!!" lol...you'll see.

while i agree he's passionate and dynamic, i can't say i really liked his teaching style. also, he's slightly psychotic and weird - why would we need a seating plan for the midterm/final? also, the tests were all MC which doesn't agree with everyone

Made 336 look easy. If you think this class is hard, take it with Dobson! Funny guy and is passionate about what he teaches. Always available for help. Midterm wasn't hard or easy either. Just pay attention in class and do you h.w and your looking at a B or B+. He always keep the class laughing and involved. Highly recommended. Love his accent.

This prof really upset me...His exam is not hard but a bit tricky and the question may not have answer! U can't get any explanation from the prof about the midterm since he just keep repeating the answer. Take George's instead of this one if u pursuit A level grade.

I like this guy's enthusiasm and passion for the course. He's right when he says this stuff is useful (he's not kidding). However, like many profs, tends to spend much of his time talking about simple concepts and not enough on the tougher ones which usually come later in his lecture; you'll find the textbook much more useful.
I'M NOT KIDDING! story of his life! Great class, great drinking game! I'M NOT KIDDING! Good way to get hammered!

He can be extremely irritating. During the final exam, in the last 20 minutes or so, he said, " I need the z-value table" about probably 200 times. He could've just projected the table up front instead of disturbing the whole class. Easiest class but annoying u know who.

"It's good stuff.  I'm not kidding, honestly."  This guy loves what he's doing and his enthusiasm rubs off on us. He's very encouraging and calls us "champions." His exams are easy and are exactly like the assignments.

He is extremely passionate about the course! He always try to make lectures more interesting and practical. He is eager to help students and always try to interact with them in class. I enjoy his class a lot. Assignments can be very time consuming but midterm was fair.

He loves this subject and is trying to make this course fun and practical. He is a hard working prof. You really can learn something that is useful in the future. Like what he said, this is a "wonderful course".

David Dobson made studying stats fun. I realize that sentence probably hasn't been said before, but he does. He is super energetic and stoked to be teaching stats. Very refreshing to see a prof that enjoys teaching, and his help/work. Don't buy text, just follow his slides and go to tutorials. Even the T.A's tell you the text is almost needless.
The course is easy in Spring 2009 but some of the assignments are harder in the summer because the TAs made those of the assignments. But still, this is an easy 336 session.
He is humourous and keeps lectures interesting. I can follow along and understand what he is teaching (I usually cannot do the same thing in other lectures).

David Dobson is practically impossible to understand; thick accent, and talks fast. He will get mad if you ask for help during office hours, and generally leave you feeling as if you're on your own. The book sucks, and does things differently than he does in class. The TA's I had are helpful, so that was good. Take it with somebody else. Good luck!
